title: Segmented Control Item
description: An enum-family variable widget rendering a horizontal segmented control: the options sit side by side as a button row, with exactly one segment active. It binds one document/container variable (the `variable` it sets) and lets a viewer pick one value from a fixed option set; on change the value flows through the resolver's override path and re-resolves the document so dependent `${var}`/`$var` consumers update live. A segmented control may only bind a variable whose declared type is `enum` — the resolver enforces this widget↔variable type compatibility and reports WIDGET_TYPE_MISMATCH otherwise. It shares the enum family's `{value,label}` option shape and ordering config with `select`. The variable itself is declared in the document/container `variables`, which supplies the effective default and the authoritative option set; this config only declares the binding, its presentation, and how the options are ordered. A segmented control suits small option sets and never has children.

constraints.description: The human label rendered beside the control — editable at runtime.
When true the control is rendered read-only — editable at runtime to gate the input.
How the option set is ordered for display — editable at runtime.

variable.description: Name of the variable this widget sets. Must be a variable visible in the widget's scope (declared at document scope or on an ancestor container) and declared with a type the widget's family permits (enum family → enum). The value chosen here becomes that variable's runtime override on re-resolve.

label.description: Optional label rendered next to the control.

description.description: Optional help text describing what the input controls, rendered beneath or beside the control.

default.description: Optional widget-local default option value pre-selected before the viewer changes the selection. The variable's declared default remains the authoritative resolution-time value; this is presentation only.

disabled.description: When true the control is rendered read-only and cannot be changed.

options.description: Selectable options, in declared display order. Each option is the value assigned to the bound variable when chosen, plus an optional human label. At least one option is required. Shares the enum family's `{value,label}` shape.

value.description: The value assigned to the bound variable when this option is selected.

label.description: Optional display label for this option; defaults to the value when omitted.

sort.description: Optional ordering applied to the displayed options. `declared` (the default) keeps the options in the order they are listed; `label` sorts ascending by display label (falling back to value); `value` sorts ascending by value.
